name: Puig de Millà Loop
name_original: Monte de Millà — 100 cimas de Cataluña
description: Hike the moderate Monte de Millà trail, a 5.2-mile route offering panoramic views and a taste of Catalonia's 100 Summits challenge.
region_intro: Hiking around Àger, located in Catalonia, Spain, offers diverse trails through a landscape shaped by the Montsec mountain range and the Noguera Ribagorçana river. The region is characterized by distinctive geological formations, including the Mont-rebei Gorge, where paths are carved directly into limestone cliffs. This area provides a variety of routes, from easy walks to more challenging ascents, catering to different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Àger The most popular hiking route is Puig de Millà Loop, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views across the landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mont-rebei Gorge – Montfalcó Walkways loop from Agulla del Forat, a difficult 10.2 miles (16.5 km) path. This trail navigates the iconic gorge with its unique carved pathways and footbridges. Local hikers also love the Coll d'Ares – Sant Alís Summit loop from Serra del Mont-sec, a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail leading through the Serra del Mont-sec, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Hiking around Àger is defined by dramatic gorges, limestone cliffs, and the Montsec mountain range. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging multi-hour treks. The routes in Àger are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Àger's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I find in Àger?
A: Àger offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from easy walks to challenging multi-hour treks. The landscape is characterized by dramatic gorges, limestone cliffs, and the Montsec mountain range, providing options for all ability levels. There are over 30 routes available, with 7 easy, 17 moderate, and 9 difficult options.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Àger?
A: Yes, Àger has several easy trails. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14943790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Ares – Sant Alís Summit loop from Serra del Mont-sec&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.7 miles (4.3 km) long and typically takes about 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This route offers accessible paths through the Serra del Mont-sec.

Q: What are some challenging hikes in the Àger region?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14945559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge – Montfalcó Walkways loop from Agulla del Forat&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.2 miles (16.5 km) path that navigates the iconic gorge with its unique carved pathways and footbridges. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085726096/rio-noguera-pallaresa-y-fontllonga-i-ametlla-circular-por-la-sierra-de-montsec-d-ares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Noguera Pallaresa y Fontllonga i Ametlla — circular por la Sierra de Montsec d'Ares&lt;/a&gt;, a 20 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Àger?
A: Yes, many trails in Àger are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932074095/puig-de-milla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de Millà Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931222901/baronia-de-sant-oisme-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baronia de Sant Oïsme Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14945559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge – Montfalcó Walkways loop from Agulla del Forat&lt;/a&gt; also offers a circular experience.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in Àger?
A: The region is famous for the stunning Mont-rebei Gorge, with paths carved directly into the rock and unique footbridges. You can also explore the Montsec mountain range. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1039653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mare de Déu de Montalegre Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/762109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port d'Àger&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Ares&lt;/a&gt;, and viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llimiana Viewpoint (Tarradets)&lt;/a&gt; overlooking the reservoirs.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Àger?
A: The trails in Àger are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 35 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic scenery of the gorges and the unique experience of paths carved into the rock, as well as the diverse options for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any opportunities for wild swimming near the hiking trails in Àger?
A: While specific wild swimming spots are not detailed for the hiking trails, the region features the Canelles reservoir and the Noguera Ribagorçana river. These bodies of water offer opportunities for activities like canoeing, and some areas might be suitable for swimming, though it's always advisable to check local regulations and safety conditions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Àger?
A: Àger is suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. While winter hiking is possible, it's important to be prepared for cooler conditions and potentially icy patches, especially at higher elevations or within the gorges. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Are there any hikes in Àger that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932074095/puig-de-milla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de Millà Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route specifically noted for its panoramic views across the landscape. Many trails within the Montsec mountain range and around the Mont-rebei Gorge also provide breathtaking vistas of the dramatic geological formations and turquoise waters.

Q: Can I find any mountain passes or summits to explore in Àger?
A: Yes, the Àger region, part of the Montsec mountain range, features several mountain passes and summits. You can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/762109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port d'Àger&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1045772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Porta&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Ares&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1045778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pala Alta Summit&lt;/a&gt; is another significant point of interest for those looking to reach higher elevations.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Àger?
A: While Àger is a popular outdoor destination, public transport options directly to all trailheads might be limited. It is generally recommended to have a car for flexibility, especially when accessing more remote starting points. Some local bus services may connect major towns, but checking specific routes and schedules in advance is advised.

Good panoramic view of the Canelles dam.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike Monte de Millà — 100 summits of Catalonia?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for Monte de Millà is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 35 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 8.4 km route with an elevation gain of about 282 meters.

faqs.question: Is the Monte de Millà trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate in difficulty. While it's part of the broader '100 Cims de Catalunya' challenge which includes very demanding peaks, this specific route to Monte de Millà is accessible to hikers with a reasonable level of fitness. It involves a moderate elevation gain and covers a distance of around 8.4 km.

faqs.question: Is the Monte de Millà hike a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for Monte de Millà is designed as a circular route, allowing you to experience varied scenery without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Monte de Millà route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several points of interest. These include the intriguing Cave of Biot del Peraire, the rewarding Monte de Millà Summit itself, and the scenic Viewpoint of Canelles Dam, offering panoramic vistas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike Monte de Millà?
faqs.answer: Given its location, the Monte de Millà trail is generally enjoyable during spring, early summer, and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. While accessible in winter, always check local weather conditions, as some sections might be colder or potentially slippery.

faqs.question: Is the Monte de Millà trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Monte de Millà trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in areas with wildlife, and to ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Monte de Millà trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Monte de Millà hike?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the Monte de Millà trailhead are not provided in the tour data. It is advisable to check local maps or community forums for designated parking areas near the starting point of the trail before your visit.

faqs.question: Does the Monte de Millà trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established trails. You will find it intersects with parts of the Montsec Bikepacking Loop 1 - La Ribagorza baja, Torres de Cas, TransCatalunya. De Ponent, and Camí de la Serra de Millà.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Monte de Millà trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this trail is varied, reflecting the diverse landscapes of Catalonia. You can expect a mix of paths through forested areas, potentially rocky sections as you approach the summit, and open stretches offering views, particularly around the Viewpoint of Canelles Dam.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for hiking Monte de Millà?
faqs.answer: While rated moderate, the ascent to the Monte de Millà Summit does involve a steady climb. An insider tip is to start your hike early in the morning, especially during warmer months, to enjoy cooler temperatures and potentially clearer views from the summit and the Viewpoint of Canelles Dam before midday haze sets in.
